hyperedges.label: End-to-End Health Ping Flow: REST /healthz → gRPC Health.Ping → pong
uv Workspace: gen + proxy + frontend packages sharing proto stubs
Three-way benchmark: REST baseline, gRPC-proxy, gRPC-direct all compared on A10G
Prompt-embeds design, corpus note, and vLLM runtime limitation form a coherent issue cluster
Modal deployment decisions: ADR 0001 environment choice feeds ADR 0002 deployment architecture

hyperedges.description: Full chat request pipeline: HTTP route translates OpenAI request to proto, grpc client sends via stub
Full completions request pipeline: HTTP route translates and forwards via gRPC client
Modal benchmark orchestration depends on pre-staged weights, the gRPC frontend serve function, and the local proxy app

hyperedges.label: Phase 2 Environment Decision: research + empirical tests + ADR → Modal A10G chosen
Phase 3 Translation Pipeline: OpenAI JSON → proto → AsyncLLM → proto → OpenAI JSON
Phase 4 CI Benchmark Strategy: FakeHTTPServer + committed baseline + benchmark.yml → regression detection
Phase 3.1 gRPC smoke test: modal_frontend_smoke.py runs proxy+frontend in container against modal_weight_volume_entity and returns smoke_test_result_entity
Phase 3.2 serve lifecycle: modal_frontend_serve_script uses modal_forward_tcp_tunnel + modal_dict_coordination to expose gRPC frontend_addr to local proxy via frontend_addr_env_var
Benchmark measures proxy overhead: x_bench_proxy_ms_header produced by bench_middleware_producer and consumed by bench_runner_consumer to populate bench_proxy_overhead_metric
bench_modal.py orchestrates REST + gRPC-proxy + gRPC-direct via shared Modal deployment
Three-way comparison pipeline: compare_three_way() → ThreeWayReport → write_three_way_md()
Two-way comparison pipeline: compare_cross() → CrossRunReport → write_cross_run_md()
Phase 5 streaming chat requires proto, SSE contract, and client API to be defined before implementation
Phase 6 completions core trio: CompletionRequest (oneof), binary encoding rationale, and wire-size benchmark
fake_frontend_server wires both FakeChatServicer and FakeCompletionsServicer for integration tests
Benchmark pipeline: corpus -> runner -> metrics -> reporter
Integration tests share fake_frontend fixture and proxy app
Comparison flow: io.load_run -> compare -> reporter output
compare regression test suite uses BenchmarkRun and RunSummary fixtures
_make_run assembles BenchmarkRun from _meta and RunSummary instances
regression threshold tests exercise compare with varying latency summaries

nodes.label: RequestResult — per-request benchmark measurement dataclass (latency, bytes, proxy_ms, ttft, tpot, request_type)
nodes.norm_label: requestresult — per-request benchmark measurement dataclass (latency, bytes, proxy_ms, ttft, tpot, request_type)

nodes.label: RunSummary — aggregated benchmark percentile summary per (target, concurrency, request_type)
nodes.norm_label: runsummary — aggregated benchmark percentile summary per (target, concurrency, request_type)

nodes.label: BenchmarkRun — top-level benchmark result container (meta + summaries + raw_results)
nodes.norm_label: benchmarkrun — top-level benchmark result container (meta + summaries + raw_results)

hyperedges.label: End-to-End Health Ping Flow: REST /healthz → gRPC Health.Ping → pong
uv Workspace: gen + proxy + frontend packages sharing proto stubs
Three-way benchmark: REST baseline, gRPC-proxy, gRPC-direct all compared on A10G
Prompt-embeds design, corpus note, and vLLM runtime limitation form a coherent issue cluster
Modal deployment decisions: ADR 0001 environment choice feeds ADR 0002 deployment architecture

hyperedges.description: Full chat request pipeline: HTTP route translates OpenAI request to proto, grpc client sends via stub
Full completions request pipeline: HTTP route translates and forwards via gRPC client
Modal benchmark orchestration depends on pre-staged weights, the gRPC frontend serve function, and the local proxy app

hyperedges.label: Phase 2 Environment Decision: research + empirical tests + ADR → Modal A10G chosen
Phase 3 Translation Pipeline: OpenAI JSON → proto → AsyncLLM → proto → OpenAI JSON
Phase 4 CI Benchmark Strategy: FakeHTTPServer + committed baseline + benchmark.yml → regression detection
Phase 3.1 gRPC smoke test: modal_frontend_smoke.py runs proxy+frontend in container against modal_weight_volume_entity and returns smoke_test_result_entity
Phase 3.2 serve lifecycle: modal_frontend_serve_script uses modal_forward_tcp_tunnel + modal_dict_coordination to expose gRPC frontend_addr to local proxy via frontend_addr_env_var
Benchmark measures proxy overhead: x_bench_proxy_ms_header produced by bench_middleware_producer and consumed by bench_runner_consumer to populate bench_proxy_overhead_metric
bench_modal.py orchestrates REST + gRPC-proxy + gRPC-direct via shared Modal deployment
Three-way comparison pipeline: compare_three_way() → ThreeWayReport → write_three_way_md()
Two-way comparison pipeline: compare_cross() → CrossRunReport → write_cross_run_md()
Phase 5 streaming chat requires proto, SSE contract, and client API to be defined before implementation
Phase 6 completions core trio: CompletionRequest (oneof), binary encoding rationale, and wire-size benchmark
fake_frontend_server wires both FakeChatServicer and FakeCompletionsServicer for integration tests
Benchmark pipeline: corpus -> runner -> metrics -> reporter
Integration tests share fake_frontend fixture and proxy app
Comparison flow: io.load_run -> compare -> reporter output
compare regression test suite uses BenchmarkRun and RunSummary fixtures
_make_run assembles BenchmarkRun from _meta and RunSummary instances
regression threshold tests exercise compare with varying latency summaries
